q: Stimulus is a detectable change in the internal or external environment which influence a temporary increase of physiological activity or response in the whole plant or any part of plant.
In the plant, there are two types of stimuli such as internal stimuli and external stimuli.
The responses to external stimuli or environmental changes that are produced by the working of various organs in a systematic and controlled manner are known as coordination.
The movement of plant parts is due to various external and internal stimuli.
External stimuli in plants include water, gravity, light intensity and duration, chemicals, temperature and touch.
Internal stimuli or internal control in plants are chemicals called plant hormones.
Plants possess a nervous system like animals to sense things around them and respond to them.
Coordination and response in plants are undertaken by plant hormones or plant growth regulators or phytohormones.
Plant responses have evolved because they provide the plant with some type of selective advantage, making them better adapted to survive and reproduce.
Examples of plant responses include the tropisms, responses to touch, responses to herbivory and responses to abiotic stress.
